Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Theyve provided a detailed description of a loan application process with strict rules normative constraints and recommended best practices desiderata Theres also an event log with three cases My task is to analyze each case against these rules and recommendations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First I need to break down the normative constraints The sequence must be Application Received Credit Check Risk Assessment Loan Decision Inform Applicant If Approved Finalize Documentation Close Case Important ordering rules Credit Check before Risk Assessment Risk Assessment before Loan Decision Inform Applicant after Loan Decision If approved Finalize Documentation before Close Case if rejected no documentation finalization before closing Skipping steps or reversing order isnt allowed and finalizing documentation after rejection is prohibited

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now looking at the desiderata Credit Check should be within 24 hours of application inform applicant within 48 hours of decision and a follow up call before closing is encouraged but not mandatory Violating these isnt severe but is suboptimal

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 The activities are in the correct order Application Received Credit Check Risk Assessment Loan Decision Approved Inform Applicant Finalize Documentation Close Case Timing looks fine Credit Check was at 0930 same day as application at 0900 so within 24 hours Inform Applicant at 1300 which is 2 hours after the decision at 1100 well within 48 hours No follow up call but thats just a desideratum So Case 1 meets all normative rules and most desiderata

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 2 Heres where it gets tricky The sequence shows Application Received then Risk Assessment then Credit Check Wait thats a problem

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The rules say Credit Check must come before Risk Assessment Here Risk Assessment happened at 0820 but Credit Check at 0900 Thats a clear violation of the ordering rule

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also the loan decision is rejected but then Finalize Documentation occurs at 1000 The rules state that if rejected documentation shouldnt be finalized before closing So thats another violation Even though Close Case follows at 1030 finalizing after rejection isnt allowed So Case 2 has two normative violations order of Credit Check and Risk Assessment and finalizing documentation after rejection

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 The sequence is correct Application Received Credit Check Risk Assessment Loan Decision Approved Inform Applicant Finalize Documentation Close Case However timing issues here Credit Check on 2024 01 14 at 1000 but application was received on 2024 01 12 at 0900 Thats more than 48 hours later actually about 49 hours Wait from Jan 12 0900 to Jan 14 1000 is over two days so definitely beyond 24 hours Thats a desideratum violation Inform Applicant on Jan 16 at 1500 but loan decision was on Jan 14 at 1200 Thats more than 48 hours later over 51 hours so another desideratum violation No follow up call but thats optional So Case 3 has no normative violations but fails on timing desiderata

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Now the user also asks for interpretations or justifications For Case 2 maybe the credit check was initiated but not completed until later but the log shows Risk Assessment before Credit Check which is still a violation Perhaps there was a system error or manual oversight For Case 3 the delays might be due to backlog or weekends but since the dates are weekdays maybe high volume or staffing issues Still these are just speculations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I should also mention that in Case 2 even though Close Case happens after Finalize Documentation the fact that documentation was finalized at all after rejection is the main issue regardless of order The rules explicitly say not to finalize documentation if rejected

Name: Validation - PE
Text: So summarizing Case 1 is fully compliant Case 2 has normative violations Case 3 has desideratum violations but no normative ones Need to present this clearly explaining each violation and the differences between strict rules and recommendations
